texto: One idea I explore in my stand-up show is whether, if you try looking at the universe rationally and avoid coping mechanisms like mysticism or religion, you can still be happy knowing you are going to die after a brief time on this spinning ball.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Robin Ince: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “One idea I explore in my stand-up show is whether, if you try looking at the universe rationally …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que fe, time, happy pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de happy e universe.

texto: If you really want space on public transport you should carry some pornography from the 1970s and a pair of children's safety scissors, then delicately cut out all the eyes of the glamour models whilst whistling. Every now and again mutter, 'Why are women more beautiful when they are eyeless?' You will be able to stretch out, though this can have ramifications such as ending up on a police list or being run out of town.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Robin Ince que provoca temas ligados a familia. Ao questionar “If you really want space on public transport you should carry some pornography from the 1970s and…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre familia, humour, reflexao, com foco em termino e reflexao.

texto: I am not man or beast; I am bibliosexual, and a seedy bibliosexual who haunts the streets, laden with carrier bags held by blistered fingers, stooping under the weight of the rucksack that has brought on sciatica and a Dickensian demeanour.
explicacao: Citação de Robin Ince sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “I am not man or beast; I am bibliosexual, and a seedy bibliosexual who haunts the streets, laden …” articula reflexao, bibliosexual, books em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como books.
